'''Steve Pickersgill''' (born 28 November 1985) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[England national rugby league team|England]] (Academy), and at club level for [[Crosfields ARLFC]] (in [[Warrington]]), and in the [[Super League]] for the [[Warrington Wolves]], and the [[Widnes Vikings]], as a {{rlp|pr}}, or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Steve Pickersgill was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England, he is the son of the rugby league {{rlp|sr}} who played in the 1970s for [[Woolston Rovers]] ARLFC (in [[Warrington]]), and [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]; [[Roy Pickersgill]].<ref name="Roy Pickersgill Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/roy-pickersgill/summary.html|title=Roy Pickersgill Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|accessdate=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Career==
Steve Pickersgill made his début for [[Warrington Wolves]] on Friday 11 March 2005, and he played his last match for Warrington Wolves during 2010's [[Super League XV]], he made his début for [[Widnes Vikings]] against [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] at [[Select Security Stadium]], [[Widnes]] on Saturday 23 May 2009, due to injuries, he retired from rugby league aged 28 during 2014's [[Super League XIX]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Steve Pickersgill: Widnes Vikings prop announces retirement|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27493571|website=BBC Sport|access-date=25 January 2015|date=20 May 2014}}</ref>
